name: MAS Boston Society
mission: Tarbiya Department Tarbiya aims at rooting the faith, purifying the soul, expanding and refining knowledge/understanding, developing and refining all aspects of character, boosting commitment, building skills, and instilling the discipline necessary to conduct a successful, fulfilling, meaningful, and balanced life of learning, virtue, commitment, and service of Islam and humanity.
theoryOfChange: Developing God-conscious community servants who can benefit all of society through spiritual training and civic engagement.

grantsData.purpose: MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.

PAYMENT TO ISB (A RELIGOUS ORGANIZATION) FOR ISB TO PAYDOWN LOAN WHICH WAS USED TO FINANCE THE CONTRUCTION OF THE ISLAMIC SOCIETY OF BOSTON CULTURAL CENTER (THE CENTER). THIS IS CONSISTENT WITH THE MANAGEMENT AGREEMENT BETWEEN MAS BOSTON AND ISB FOR THE CENTER.

MONEY COLLECTED AS PART OF ZAKAT (AN EXPECTATION IN THE MUSLIM FAITH THAT ALL FINANCIALLY STABLE MUSLIMS PAY MONEY TOWARDS HELPING NEEDY INDIVIDUALS IN THE COMMUNITY) IS GIVEN TO THE ISLAMIC CIRCLE OF NORTH AMERICA (ICNA) RELIEF, WHO THEN DISTRIBUTES THIS MONEY BASED ON AN APPLICATION AND REVIEW PROCESS TO NEEDY INDIVIDUALS.
PAYMENT TO ISB FOR ISB TO PAY DOWN THE CULTURAL CENTER (MOSQUE) LOAN.

dimension_explanations.alignment: The charity shows high alignment for Muslim donors as a zakat-eligible entity that addresses religious and cultural urgency through civic engagement and spiritual education [1][3]. Its fundraising efficiency of $0.05 per $1 raised reflects a disciplined approach to resource management [2].
dimension_explanations.impact: MAS Boston demonstrates a commitment to capacity building through its Tarbiya and Youth Development programs, maintaining a high program expense ratio of 85.3% [4]. While it operates with a total revenue of $3,286,832, its working capital is relatively lean at 3.2 months [2][4].

alignment.explanation: Alignment is high due to <cite id="1">explicit zakat programs</cite> serving categories like the poor and needy, and its focus on a <cite id="12">niche religious-cultural cause area</cite>.

credibility.explanation: Donors can find confidence in MAS Boston's <cite id="9">strong board governance</cite> and its long-standing presence in the community since 2005. The organization maintains <cite id="13">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and clear reporting of executive compensation.
credibility.improvement: To strengthen credibility, the organization should <cite id="10">publish annual impact reports</cite> that include specific beneficiary data and audited financial statements.

Value organizations that also support other local Muslim nonprofits through grants
Seek to fulfill zakat obligations within the Boston Muslim community
giving_considerations: Gifts support a mix of direct youth programming and community grantmaking

Focus is regional (Boston) rather than international

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that while programs are active and reported, there is a lack of rigorous external evaluation or long-term outcome tracking.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: MAS Boston utilizes a 'Tarbiya' framework to develop character and spiritual maturity, which is intended to lead to community service and civic engagement.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that spiritual and leadership programs are effectively achieving their goals of character development.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With a <cite id="8">13.4% revenue growth rate</cite> and a broad mission, the organization has significant capacity to expand its regional programs.

strengths.detail: The organization directs <cite id="4">86.0% of its total expenses</cite> toward its core programs, exceeding the industry standard of 75%.
MAS Boston demonstrates lean operations by spending only <cite id="5">$0.05 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions.
The charity has shown strong financial momentum with a <cite id="8">3-year revenue CAGR of 13.4%</cite>.

strengths_deep_dive: MAS Boston's financial health is anchored by its ability to maintain high program spending while growing its resource base. With <cite id="3">revenue reaching $3,286,832</cite>, the organization has successfully scaled its 'Tarbiya' and youth initiatives. Its governance is supported by a <cite id="9">strong board of 7 members</cite>, ensuring oversight of its <cite id="6">$332,783 grantmaking portfolio</cite> which strengthens the broader Boston Muslim ecosystem.
rich_narrative.summary: The Muslim American Society - MAS Boston serves as a vital community hub dedicated to 'Tarbiya,' a comprehensive process of <cite id="1">spiritual and character development</cite> designed to foster a balanced life of service. Operating primarily in the Greater Boston area, the organization manages a diverse portfolio of programs including <cite id="2">Youth Development, Public Affairs & Civic Engagement (PACE), and the Arkanum program</cite>. Financially, the organization demonstrates significant scale for a regional religious entity, reporting <cite id="3">total revenue of $3,286,832</cite> in its most recent comprehensive filing. A defining feature of MAS Boston's operations is its high level of fiscal efficiency, maintaining a <cite id="4">program expense ratio of 85.3%</cite>, which ensures that the vast majority of donor contributions directly support community services rather than administrative overhead. Furthermore, the organization exhibits <cite id="5">strong fundraising efficiency</cite>, spending only $0.05 to raise every $1. Beyond direct services, MAS Boston acts as a significant community grantmaker, distributing <cite id="6">$332,783 in domestic grants</cite> to local partners such as the Islamic Society of Boston and the Islamic Circle of North America. This dual role as both a service provider and a community funder positions MAS Boston as a central pillar of the regional Muslim infrastructure, facilitating <cite id="7">zakat-eligible programming</cite> that serves the poor (fuqara) and needy (masakin).
